title: Standing version-bracketed regression red-set (FW-C)
description: One entry per historical fix, each a shape proven RED on the released rolldown BEFORE the fix and GREEN AT/after it. Run by scripts/regression-redset.ts against both the red and green npm targets (a local PR snapshot for RED-0) so anything that ever broke twice can never silently break a third time. See .agents/docs/regression-red-set.md.

greenSnapshot.note: PR-10104 strict-order arc snapshot; the RED-0 green target. Override with REDSET_GREEN_SNAPSHOT.

entries.cluster: 1.2 wrapped-ESM init emission — cross-chunk init cycle
entries.title: cross-chunk init_* cycle (barrel/CJS)

provenance.fixCommit: no released main fix — GREEN only on final-snapshot-42628c18b (PR-10104 strict-order arc); still OPEN on npm 1.1.5
provenance.note: Generator shape buildCrossChunkInitCycle (src/generate.ts). Also the live-catch acceptance scripts/cross-chunk-init-cycle-catch.ts + .agents/evidence/9887-cross-chunk-init-cycle.json.

provenance.fixCommit: 63fbc96e1 fix: avoid dangling wrapped-ESM init call across chunks (#9502) (#9717)
6345ddf70 fix(deconflict): rename CJS-wrapped locals that shadow chunk-root bindings (#9921)

entries.cluster: 1.2 wrapped-ESM init emission — initialize re-export owners
entries.title: wrapped-ESM re-export owner not initialized (createIcon)

provenance.note: FW-B deliverable 5. Verified runtime witness (entry2 CJS-requires the ESM createIcon.js, wrapping it as init_createIcon; entry1 reads createIcon through reexport.js, but on 1.0.1 the re-export owner's init_createIcon() is not emitted before the forwarded createIcon('Arrow') runs, so SvgIcon is undefined -> TypeError). Also GREEN on current 1.1.5. Fixture: upstream issues/8950, translated to the JS API.

entries.title: dynamic entry merged into common chunk with cjs+esm wrap kind
entries.expectedRedSignature: raw-crash:["SyntaxError","Export 'imp_exports' is not defined in module"]

provenance.fixCommit: fix: dynamic entry merged into common chunk with cjs and esm wrap kind (#7771)
provenance.note: FW-B deliverable 5. BRACKET CORRECTION: the mining doc listed a pessimistic 'pre-v1.0.0-beta (git-bisect a nightly)' bracket, but the fix landed exactly at the published beta.58 -> beta.59 boundary — no nightly bisect needed. Verified runtime witness: the dynamically-imported CJS entry `imp` and the ESM `imp2` merge into one common chunk carrying BOTH a cjs wrap (require_imp) and an esm wrap (init_imp2); on beta.58 the merged chunk's export list references an undefined `imp_exports`, so Node throws SyntaxError when the dynamic import resolves that chunk. Green on beta.59 and current 1.1.5. Fixture: upstream optimization/chunk_merging/dynamic_entry_merged_in_common_chunk2.

provenance.fixCommit: 5a5f8f5d7 fix: pick dominator for runtime placement to avoid cycles (#9164)
provenance.note: FW-B deliverable 5. BRACKET CORRECTION: the mining doc listed rc.15 -> rc.16, but the #9164 fix commit (5a5f8f5d7, 2026-04-26) is not in any release before rc.18 (rc.15/16/17 all predate it, and rc.16 already throws). Verified: rc.17 RED / rc.18 GREEN. Runtime witness: with experimental.chunkOptimization the runtime helper __exportAll is placed into the node1 chunk and entry-2 imports it back, closing an entry-2<->node1 cycle; loading entry-0 runs node1's top-level __exportAll(...) before the defining chunk -> TypeError. Green on rc.18 (helper placed in the dominator) and current 1.1.5. Fixture: upstream issues/8920_2, converted to a runtime value assertion.

entries.cluster: chunking — manual codeSplitting cross-entry execution leak (seo:false)
entries.title: cross-entry execution leak without strictExecutionOrder

provenance.fixPr: OPEN — no fix released; #9997 fixed only the strictExecutionOrder:true path, the seo:false leak is still open
provenance.fixCommit: none — bracket-pending: the bug is RED on BOTH npm 1.1.5 AND the final-snapshot-42628c18b (open everywhere at seo:false), so there is no green target yet; the entry graduates to a normal bracket when a fix lands and the green target flips
provenance.note: The fuzzer-model translation of the issue's verified repro (buildCrossEntryLeakCase, src/generate.ts), caught by the reachability-isolation oracle the persisted seo:false axis selects. Live-catch acceptance: scripts/cross-entry-leak-catch.ts + .agents/evidence/9998-cross-entry-leak.json.
provenance.broaderShapeFoldedIn: FW-B deliverable 4 folded the W14c-residual 'broader automatic-chunking cross-entry leak' here: same ROOT mechanism as #9998 (a co-locating organic group at seo:false runs a disjoint-reachability entry's top-level), only a broader TRIGGER, so NOT a separate bracket. buildStaticCrossEntryLeak (src/generate.ts) + scripts/broad-cross-entry-leak-catch.ts — isolation RED 20/20 on BOTH npm 1.1.5 AND the snapshot, seo:true control GREEN; evidence .agents/evidence/broad-cross-entry-leak.json.
provenance.broaderShapeAblation: The W14c 'automatic chunking' characterization is DISPROVEN: automatic chunking does NOT leak (GREEN on both targets; 0/28 random automatic multi-entry seo:false cases). A co-locating organic group is load-bearing. The genuinely broader surface is PURELY STATIC (no dynamic import) under a PLAIN organic group (not only entriesAware): two mutually-unreachable entries sharing a static module, each owning a private eager module; loading ONE runs the OTHER's private top-level (reachability-isolation:[le2-b,le2-pb]).
